mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of durable goods and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and business demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, or single-series effects dominate the joint signal in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side capacity utilization and earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions, or the correlation reverses sign in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4 percent monthly are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate tests.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.1 million units signal strong residential investment and construction sector earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during recovery phases.
failure_condition: HOUST values above 1.1 million fail to predict positive 5-day returns in recovery regimes, or the signal reverses direction in walk-forward validation.
